authors_affiliation: Yang Liu, Kuanquan Wang, Yongfeng Yuan, Qince Li, Yacong Li, Yongpeng Xu, Henggui Zhang; Harbin Institute of Technology (HIT), Peng Cheng Laboratory, Yongjia County Public Security Bureau, University of Manchester, Southwest Medical University

result.evaluation_split: 5-fold cross validation on the official training data only; paper explicitly states the team was unable to score/rank on the official hidden test set and that these training-set results may be over-optimistic

self_reported_metrics.other: 5-fold CV averages: AUPRC 0.5615, F2 0.5765, G2 0.3240, challenge metric 0.5501 +/- 0.0223

model.architecture_details: Residual CNN (9 residual blocks, each with 2 convolutional layers plus batch norm/dropout/ReLU, filter count increasing and kernel size halving every 3 blocks, down-sampled by max-pooling) feeding into a class-wise attention module based on Transformer-style scaled dot-product attention (one-hot class encodings as queries, linearly transformed local features as keys, local features as values), followed by per-class fully-connected sigmoid output layers.
model.key_novelty: A class-wise attention mechanism that generates a separate attended feature vector per class (using class identities as attention queries) to reduce inter-class conflict in multi-label ECG classification, combined with a novel weighted focal loss for class imbalance.

input.signal_length_window: 60 seconds fixed length via random truncation or zero-padding
input.preprocessing: Amplitude scaled to a common resolution (1000/mV), resampled to 250 Hz, moving-average filter (window 250) for baseline wander removal, 3-level 'db4' wavelet transform with soft-thresholding for denoising, truncation/zero-padding to 60 seconds

data.training_datasets: PhysioNet/Computing in Cardiology Challenge 2020 official public training set (43101 ECG recordings from six databases across 4 institutions, 111 label types, 24 scored classes used); no external data reported
data.class_imbalance_handling: Novel weighted focal loss with class-frequency-dependent alpha weighting terms (alpha_i,0 and alpha_i,1 based on N/(N-Ni) and N/Ni raised to tunable power b, set to b=0.5)
data.augmentation_techniques: not reported (only random truncation/padding position noted, not framed as augmentation)

training.loss_function: Weighted focal loss (class-frequency-weighted extension of focal loss)
training.cross_validation_strategy: 5-fold cross validation on the training dataset
training.hardware: Workstation with one CPU at 3.5 GHz, NVIDIA Quadro K6000 GPU, 64 GB memory; implemented in Keras

contribution.summary: Proposes a residual CNN combined with a Transformer-inspired class-wise attention mechanism (using class identities as attention queries) plus a weighted focal loss, to address multi-label conflicts and class imbalance in 12-lead ECG classification.
